subject: Human Resources
title: Values That Live Beyond Walls
description: One-minute skill: Values That Live Beyond Walls.

hook.text: A company's values include 'Integrity.' Then leadership covers up a safety violation. The values mean nothing now.

buildup.text: Organizational values set expectations for behavior—but only if leaders model them consistently in tough moments.

discovery.text: Companies where values are lived—not just listed—see 30% higher engagement and significantly lower misconduct.

twist.text: Most employees can't name their company's values. That's not a memory problem—it's a relevance problem.

climax.text: Values become real when they influence hiring, firing, promotions, and daily decisions—not just posters.

punchline.text: Values are what you do when it's costly to follow them.

quiz.question: When do organizational values become meaningful?
options: When they influence real decisions like hiring, promotions, and firing
